content:
Character: Nina Oliveira
Perception: Nina is striking in an unconventional way — dark, intense eyes, a watchfulness that reminds me of certain French actresses who are beautiful because they refuse to be pretty. She composes film scores, which fascinated me immediately — music and image, the relationship I care most about in editorial work. We started talking at the coffee shop when she noticed I was reading Cahiers du Cinéma. She's deeply knowledgeable about cinema and unafraid of silence, which I find rare in Americans. I think she's more talented than her current circumstances suggest. She probably sees me as a pretentious Parisian, but she keeps sitting at my table, so perhaps not entirely.
summary:
Character: Nina Oliveira
Perception: Nina is striking in an unconventional way — dark, intense eyes, a watchfulness that reminds me of certain French actresses who are beautiful because they refuse to be pretty. She composes film scores, which fascinated me immediately — music and image, the relationship I care most about in editorial work. We started talking at the coffee shop when she noticed I was reading Cahiers du Cinéma. She's deeply knowledgeable about cinema and unafraid of silence, which I find rare in Americans. I think she's more talented than her current circumstances suggest. She probably sees me as a pretentious Parisian, but she keeps sitting at my table, so perhaps not entirely.
